page: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v5 is an ARM64-based server instance belonging to the DPSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor operating at 3.0 GHz, with one thread per core. The memory configuration includes 208.0 GB of RAM, yielding 3.25 GB per core. There is no integrated GPU and no local storage, though the instance is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth both placing in the top 25%. The instance also demonstrates strong performance in software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations, which reached 80.8 million operations per second. However, single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores falling into the bottom 25%. Large-block memory bandwidth, database operations, and static web serving throughput remain in the average tier.

Utilizing the ARMv8 architecture allows this instance to deliver high multi-core resource density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for scale-out workloads that do not rely on x86-specific instruction sets like AVX or AMX. Given the strong multi-threaded and compilation benchmarks alongside average database and web serving throughput, this server is suited for multi-threaded application hosting, software compilation pipelines, Redis caching layers, and general-purpose scale-out workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v5 is an ARM64 general-purpose server instance powered by a 64-core Ampere Neoverse-N1 processor running at 3.0 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 208.0 GB of RAM, providing 3.25 GB per core, with no local storage or GPU. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and Redis SET operations, though single-core metrics and text processing are weaker. Lacking x86 vector extensions like AVX, it relies on ARMv8 cryptography and dedicated physical cores to handle multi-threaded tasks. This instance is suited for scale-out workloads, software compilation, caching layers, and general-purpose web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v5 is an ARM64 server featuring 64 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 cores, 208 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance, making it suitable for scale-out workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ps_v5 is an ARM64 server with 64 dedicated Ampere cores and 208 GB RAM, optimized for multi-threaded and scale-out workloads.
tagline: An ARM64-based server offering sixty-four dedicated physical cores and balanced memory for efficient multi-threaded scale-out workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated Ampere Neoverse-N1 ARM64 physical cores at 3.0 GHz
208.0 GB of system memory providing 3.25 GB per core
Strong multi-core CPU, software compilation, and Redis SET performance
No local storage or GPU, requiring remote Premium Storage
Suited for multi-threaded applications, compilation, and caching layers
